title: lora_l2.c + lichen_l2.c: lichen_lora_l2_start idempotent but enable path may double-register callback
description:
Cross-file interaction inconsistency: lichen_lora_l2_start() is idempotent (returns 0 if already running), but lichen_l2_enable(true) unconditionally registers RX callback.

lora_l2.c:610-611: start() returns 0 immediately if state==RUNNING

lichen_l2.c:1113: ret = lichen_lora_l2_set_rx_callback(lora_rx_callback, NULL);
lichen_l2.c:1128: ret = lichen_lora_l2_start();

SCENARIO:
1. System running, link_ctx_initialized=1, callback registered
2. Something calls lichen_l2_enable(true) again
3. Line 1061 checks link_ctx_initialized, it's already 1, skips init block
4. Line 1113 registers callback again (idempotent, just overwrites)
5. Line 1128 calls start(), returns 0 (already running)

This is SAFE but wasteful. The set_rx_callback at line 1113 is unconditional even when already running.

P4: Not a bug, just minor inefficiency. Could add 'if (!is_running())' guard around callback registration.
close_reason: not-a-bug: idempotent operations, minor inefficiency only
